name: Calas
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small mixing bowl, thoroughly mix the flour, sugar, salt, and nutmeg.
2. Add active dry yeast to the mixture and stir until combined.
3. Gradually add water, beating with an electric mixer at medium speed for 2 minutes, scraping the bowl occasionally.
4. Stir in the eggs and enough flour to make a thick batter; beat at high speed for 1 minute, scraping the bowl frequently.
5. Add the cooked rice and continue beating for 1 more minute.
6. Mix in additional flour until the dough is very soft.
7. Cover and let the dough rise in a warm, draft-free place until doubled in bulk, about 30 minutes.
8. Heat oil to 370°F (190°C).
9. Stir batter gently; drop by tablespoon into hot oil and fry once, turning, until deep golden brown, about 3 minutes.
10. Drain on paper towels and roll in sugar before serving warm.
NOTE: Until recent years, women selling calas were daily-morning figures on the streets of New Orleans. Upon hearing their cries, "Belle calas, tout chaud," Creole cooks rushed out to get them fresh.
